Record Meeting captures Google Meet, Zoom, Teams, Webex, Telegram, WhatsApp, and Discord calls in one click, transcribes them automatically, and turns every meeting into searchable notes, summaries, and action items. No extra bot joins the call. Free to start.

## Forum Threads

RecordMeetingp/recordmeetingNathan•

26d ago [...] Promoted

Nathan

RecordMeeting

RecordMeeting

📌

Hey Product Hunt! Nathan here, founder of Qualtir. We built Record Meeting because most AI notetakers still send a bot into your call. That awkward "Notetaker has joined" moment kills the vibe on sales calls, interviews, and internal meetings. Record Meeting works differently. One click inside Google Meet. No bot joins the room. Full video, transcript, and AI summary after the call. Free tier gives you 20 recordings to try on real meetings. I'd love your feedback. What meeting would you record first? Sales, hiring, or internal sync?

Saul Fleischman

RiteKit Company Logo API

RiteKit Company Logo API

@nthglsn 🦊 Foxy picked your launch out of today's batch. [...] Report

Burhan

Finally something that works across Meet and Teams without inviting another bot into the room. The auto summary pulled out the right action items from a 40 minute call, which was a nice surprise.

# RecordMeetingAlternatives

Looking beyond RecordMeeting? Try Granola for an AI notepad that captures action items, MeetGeek for auto summaries and shareable highlights, Grain for team-wide recordings, Bluedot for privacy-first notes, and tl;dv to skim meetings in minutes.

## Products used by RecordMeeting

### Explore the tech stack and tools that power RecordMeeting. See what products RecordMeeting uses for development, design, marketing, analytics, and more.

OpenRouter Model Fusion [...] OpenRouter Model Fusion

OpenRouter Model FusionRun many models side by side and fuse the best answer

5.0 (35 reviews)

Unified API

We route all AI summary generation through OpenRouter, which lets us run Gemini under the OpenAI-compatible API without rewriting our clients. Having one unified endpoint with model fallback flexibility was a massive quality-of-life win at scale.

Cloudflare

CloudflareThe web performance & security company

5.0 (192 reviews)

Cloud Computing Platforms

Three Cloudflare products power the core recording pipeline: R2 stores every raw video and HLS segment, a Workers streaming worker orchestrates the video-processing queue, and Cloudflare handles our global edge delivery. R2 alone replaced the need to self-manage an S3 equivalent, with zero egress costs. [...] Firebase

FirebaseAn app development platform backed by Google

5.0 (229 reviews)

Engineering & Development

•

Authentication & identity tools

•

Databases and backend frameworks

Firebase hosts the web app, runs all our backend Cloud Functions, and Firestore stores every meeting, transcript, and user record. Deploying a full backend without managing servers was the right call for a small team moving fast.

Subscribe

results.title: Backtrack 2.0: Record any meeting backwards & generate AI notes. | Product Hunt
results.content:
SocialX

4.9

Based on 17 reviews

Review Backtrack 2.0?

Reviewers describe Backtrack 2.0 as a practical memory aid that removes the need to remember to hit record, then helps them revisit meetings, interviews, Slack huddles, Discord chats, and even on-screen activity from earlier in the day. The most repeated praise is workflow relief: less frantic note-taking, easier recaps, and faster follow-up with recordings, transcripts, and summaries. Users also repeatedly credit the team for listening and shipping improvements quickly. Criticism is light but specific: requests for compression and timestamped comments, plus one report of Apple TV+ video turning black while the app runs.

Backtrack is a meeting recording app that overwrites a 5 hour window of audio/video over and over throughout the day, locally on your Mac. It allows you to privately save anything you've seen, said, or heard in the past and turn it into AI notes/summaries.

Congratulations on the launch. Great product and I believe the ability to choose what to record, when and who gets access is very useful when one is working with large cross-functional teams in different timezones. I haven't gotten to this point yet, but - does the app also allow for compression(size) of the recorded video? as a feature recommendation(may be you already have this on your roadmap) - it would be great if we were allowed to timestamp with our comments/POV, so the content we record becomes so much more valuable, instantly.

results.title: Grain: AI-powered meeting recording for everyone, not just sales. | Product Hunt
results.content:
4.8

Based on 16 reviews

Review Grain?

Reviewers mostly see Grain as a reliable meeting recorder that saves time after calls. They repeatedly praise fast transcription, detailed summaries, clear action items, easy search across past conversations, and useful AI features like transcript chat and CRM updates. Several say it stands out against Zoom, Fireflies, and even Gong for note quality and follow-up workflows. A recurring use case is sales and customer or UX interviews, where clips and transcripts make it easy to pull exact moments. The main downside mentioned is occasional technical glitches.

+13

Summarized with AI

Pros

Cons

Reviews

All Reviews

Most Informative

Pros

meeting recording(6)

saves time(5)

actionable insights(3)

team collaboration(3) [...] Product Hunt

Product Hunt

•29 reviews

#### What's great

meeting recording (6)collaboration tools (2)actionable insights (3)saves time (5)

My favorite tool of the category, and always my go-to. Great for UX research, and sales / discovery calls. Solid, great, smooth, thoughtful interface plus I've been super impressed with the new AI features - summarization, etc.

#### What needs improvement

I miss the in-meeting note-taker. I'm not sure why they removed it, but it was a really helpful way to call out important moments during the interview.

#### vs Alternatives

Fireflies.ai  👔Fireflies.ai 👔FathomFathomGranolaGranola [...] Grain genuinely nails what it set out to do: let you actually listen and engage during meetings instead of furiously typing like you're in a court hearing. The real magic happens with the transcription—it's crisp, accurate, and clips itself beautifully. What really impressed me was the ability to take time-stamped notes and turn them into actual clips within the meeting.
